description: An antifungal medicine for yeast infections such as thrush (Candida) and for cryptococcal meningitis. It blocks a fungal enzyme needed to build the cell membrane and is well absorbed by mouth.

halfLife.conditions: terminal plasma elimination half-life 'approximately 30 hours (range: 20–50 hours)' after oral administration (FDA label). Predominantly renal elimination (~80% unchanged in urine). The long half-life is the teachable feature (see notes: loading-dose rationale).

vd.conditions: the FDA label states the apparent volume of distribution 'approximates that of total body water' rather than printing a number. Total body water is ~0.6 L/kg; fluconazole's low (11–12%) protein binding and wide tissue penetration place its reported volume slightly higher, ~0.7 L/kg, the value used (band 0.6–0.8 L/kg). Scaled to absolute litres against the 70 kg reference subject at derivation time.

F.conditions: oral bioavailability is 'over 90%' compared with intravenous administration, and is not affected by food (FDA label). Point value 0.90. Because F is measured (~1), the oral and IV curves nearly coincide apart from the absorption phase.

tmax.conditions: time to peak plasma concentration is 'between 1 and 2 hours' after oral dosing in fasted subjects (FDA label). Representative point value 1.5 h.

fda_label.title: DIFLUCAN (fluconazole) tablets / injection — FDA Structured Product Label, Clinical Pharmacology (terminal half-life ~30 h, range 20–50 h; volume of distribution approximates total body water; oral bioavailability over 90%; Tmax 1–2 h; protein binding 11–12%; plasma concentrations and AUC dose proportional)

notes:
A clean LINEAR one-compartment antifungal, curated from the FDA Diflucan label. Reported PK: terminal half-life ~30 h (20–50 h), volume of distribution approximating total body water, oral bioavailability over 90% (food-independent), Tmax 1–2 h, protein binding 11–12%, and plasma concentrations/AUC that are explicitly dose-proportional — so superposition is on firm ground.

TEACHING VALUE — the loading dose. Fluconazole's ~30 h half-life means ~5–6 days to reach steady state on a fixed daily maintenance dose; the label's clinical guidance to give a loading dose of twice the daily maintenance dose on day 1 is the canonical illustration of why a long-half-life drug is loaded. The recurring-dose view (a day-1 double dose then daily maintenance) shows the accumulation-to-plateau this shortcuts. (The app plots illustrative curves for user-chosen doses; it does not recommend a regimen — the bright line holds.)

VOLUME: the label gives the volume qualitatively as 'approximates total body water.' Total body water is ~0.6 L/kg; the value used is ~0.7 L/kg (band 0.6–0.8) to reflect fluconazole's slightly wider distribution at low protein binding. Because F ~ 1 the apparent-vs-true-volume distinction is negligible.

ROUTES: oral (F ~0.9) and IV are both real products; the IV form is given as an infusion (iv_infusion available), with iv_bolus offered illustratively (F = 1 by definition).

RENAL ELIMINATION: ~80% excreted unchanged renally — clearance scales with renal function (an educational point, not a patient-specific adjustment). Educational illustration only — not for dosing.
